part1.title: Call from the Sales Team
part1.description: The sales team just called: there's a highly contingent deal in the pipeline that would significantly increase your {exposure_type} exposure. The deal has a 50/50 chance of closing next quarter.

choices.label: Hedge with forward/future
choices.description: Lock in the rate now. If the deal falls through, you'll face breakage costs.

choices.label: Hedge with option
choices.description: Pay a premium for flexibility. No breakage if the deal falls through.

choices.label: Don't hedge yet
choices.description: Wait and see if the deal materialises before committing.

part2_materialised.title: The Deal Closed!
part2_materialised.description: The contingent deal has materialised. Your exposure has increased.

hedge_forward_itm.description: Your forward is in the money. Mixed reactions from the board.

board_reactions.praise: Smart move hedging early — the rate moved in our favour.
board_reactions.criticism: You got lucky. Hedging a contingent deal with a forward was reckless. What if it hadn't closed?

hedge_forward_otm.description: Your forward is out of the money. The board is not impressed.

board_reactions.criticism: You hedged a 50/50 deal with a hard commitment and it's underwater. That's poor risk management.

hedge_option_itm.description: Your option is in the money. The board acknowledges the strategy.

board_reactions.praise: The option worked out. Good use of flexible instruments for contingent exposure.

hedge_option_otm.description: Your option expired worthless, but the deal closed and you're exposed.

board_reactions.criticism: We paid a premium and got nothing. At least there's no breakage cost.

no_hedge.description: The deal closed and you're fully exposed to market rates.

board_reactions.criticism: The deal closed and we have no hedge in place. Rates have moved against us.

part2_not_materialised.title: Deal Fell Through
part2_not_materialised.description: The contingent deal did not materialise.

hedge_forward.description: You hedged a deal that never happened. Must unwind at breakage cost.

board_reactions.criticism: We now have a hedge with no underlying exposure. Unwind costs are significant.

hedge_option.description: The option simply expires. Premium was the cost of prudence.

board_reactions.mild_criticism: We spent money on options for a deal that never closed.
board_reactions.grudging_praise: At least you used options — no breakage cost. That was smart.

no_hedge.description: Good call — no hedge needed for a deal that didn't happen.

board_reactions.praise: Prudent decision to wait. No unnecessary costs incurred.

events.name: Revenue Shortfall
events.title: Revenue Warning
events.description: Sales have come in 20% below forecast this quarter. Your revenue exposures have been reduced accordingly. Review your hedge book — you may now be over-hedged and should consider unwinding excess positions yourself.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the revenue shortfall and review your book.

events.name: Cost Overrun
events.title: Cost Overrun Alert
events.description: Operating costs have exceeded budget by 15%. Your cost-side commodity and FX exposures have grown beyond your existing hedges. You'll need to add coverage yourself to avoid an under-hedged book.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the cost overrun and rebalance your hedges.

events.name: Major Margin Call
events.title: Margin Call — Additional Collateral Required
events.description: Market moves have triggered a significant margin call on your derivative positions. Cash must be posted immediately.

choices.label: Post the margin
choices.description: Use cash reserves to meet the margin call.

choices.label: Partially unwind positions
choices.description: Close some positions to reduce margin requirements. Incurs breakage costs.

events.name: Regulatory Change
events.title: New Regulatory Requirements
events.description: The regulator has mandated stricter margin requirements on OTC derivatives. Your posting requirements just doubled.

choices.label: Comply immediately
choices.description: Post additional margin. Strains cash but keeps regulators happy.

choices.label: Restructure positions
choices.description: Move to exchange-traded products to reduce margin. Takes time and costs.

events.name: Product Not Approved
events.title: FDA Rejection — Product Not Approved
events.description: Your blockbuster drug has been rejected by the regulator. Projected USD revenue has evaporated — your revenue exposures have been wiped out. Any forward hedges you booked against this revenue are now naked positions; you'll need to unwind them yourself (and pay the breakage). Options would have simply expired.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the rejection and review your hedge book.

events.board_note: Options used for revenue hedging are praised here — no breakage. Forwards are criticised.

events.name: Project Delay
events.title: Major Project Delayed 6 Months
events.description: Your flagship project has been delayed by 6 months. Near-term materials and equipment exposures have shrunk by half — the work simply isn't happening yet. Your existing hedges still settle on the original schedule, leaving you over-hedged in the short term. Roll or unwind to match the new timeline.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the delay and rebalance your hedges.

events.name: Fuel Price Spike
events.title: Fuel Prices Surge 25%
events.description: Geopolitical tensions have caused a 25% spike in fuel prices. Brent has just jumped — your unhedged fuel exposure is now marked at a much higher level. Decide whether to lock in here or ride it out.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the fuel spike and review your hedge book.

events.name: Private Equity Takeover
events.title: Private Equity Acquisition
events.description: The company has been acquired by a PE firm. Significant debt has been added. The board has been replaced with PE partners who have zero tolerance for losses. Your IR exposure just doubled.

choices.label: Adapt to new reality
choices.description: Adjust your strategy for the new debt load and tougher board.

events.name: Counterparty Downgrade
events.title: Bank Counterparty Downgraded
events.description: One of your banking counterparties has been downgraded by rating agencies. Your concentrated positions with them are now a concern.

choices.label: Move positions to other banks
choices.description: Transfer hedges to stronger counterparties. Incurs transfer costs.

choices.label: Maintain existing positions
choices.description: Keep positions where they are. Risk of counterparty failure.

events.name: Supply Chain Disruption
events.title: Supply Chain Disruption
events.description: A major supply route has been disrupted. Your commodity prices have spiked 15%. Existing hedges have rallied in your favour, but any new coverage will be more expensive — review your book and decide whether to add more.

choices.label: Continue
choices.description: Acknowledge the disruption and review your hedges.
